world.title: Acorn Foundation Supporting Community Initiatives (New Zealand)
world.description: Deadline: 30-Apr-2026 The Acorn Foundation 2026 Funding Round provides grants of up to $10,000 to registered charities and community organisations in New Zealand’s Western Bay of Plenty. It supports initiatives in housing, youth, mental health, equity, Māori-led development, and environmental sustainability, and funds both project and operational costs. The programme aims to strengthen long-term community

world.title: Joe Exotic, The Tiger King, is still in prison and still making headlines somehow
world.description: Joe Exotic rose to fame through Tiger King, but his prison life keeps him in the spotlight. Find out what is driving his ongoing legal fights and media buzz.

world.title: World Bank warns of vulnerability to Middle East shock
world.description: Ghana is among the African economies most exposed to escalating conflict in the Middle East while having limited fiscal space to respond, according to the World Bank’s latest Africa Economic Update as government moved to contain a sharp rise in...

world.title: Trump attacks Pope over criticism of Iran war
world.description: The US president accuses Leo of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy".

world.title: Why Botswana’s Okavango Delta Leads Eco Travel in Africa
world.description: Rising interest in low-impact safaris is putting Botswana’s Okavango Delta in the spotlight, as travelers seek intact wilderness, community partnerships and science-led conservation.

world.title: EU activates fully digital Entry/Exit System
world.description: The European Union announced on Friday that the Entry/Exit System is now fully operational across all Schengen Area countries, marking a key step in strengthening border security.

world.title: Don’t Diminish the UN Peacekeeping Mission in South Sudan
world.description: This week, the United Nations Security Council will debate the renewal of the UN peacekeeping mission in South Sudan (UNMISS) as civilians throughout the country are at risk of atrocities and need greater protection.

world.title: 'Peerless master': Video of female Hong Kong cop subduing snake with her hands goes viral
world.description: A video of a female Hong Kong police sergeant subduing a 1.6-metre long snake lurking in a cabinet has gone viral.In the video posted on Sunday (April 12), three police officers are seen reaching into a fallen cabinet.Two male officers, with their backs to the camera, are each holding a bag and a wiper stick respectively. They are seen retracting...

business.title: ATCL Targets 2027 Break-Even As Fleet Growth Fuels Revenue
business.description: Air Tanzania aims for break-even by 2027, leveraging a modernized fleet and strategic route expansion. The goal resets East Africa's aviation hierarchy.

business.title: 'Quite some time' before fuel supply returns to normal
business.description: The Chief Executive of the Convenience Stores and Newsagents Association, which represents over 1,500 retailers including 300 filling stations, has warned it is going to take "quite some time" before things return to normal.

business.title: Oil jumps above $100 after failed peace talks, forint surges after the Hungarian election results
business.description: Oil prices surged and stock markets dipped after reports that the US may move to block the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile in Europe, the Hungarian forint rose following the election results.

business.title: The Countdown Is On: Aussie Millions Returns to Crown with CrownBet and Pepperstone Trading
business.description: **media[1010404]**MELBOURNE, Australia, April 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The countdown to one of the world's most iconic poker tournaments is officially underway, with the 2026 Aussie Millions Poker Championship returning to Crown Melbourne this month - now presented by CrownBet and backed by Pepperstone Trading as a major partner.**media[1010405]**

business.title: China's CATL, Jianlong to scale truck electrification
business.description: China's largest battery manufacturer CATL and private-sector steel group Jianlong signed a strategic co-operation agreement on 10 April, aimed at accelerating the decarbonisation of steelmaking and mining through greater electrification and renewable energy deployment.

science.title: NASA’s Artemis II crew returns to Earth
science.description: NASA's historic 10-day mission around the moon came to an end on Friday, as the Artemis II astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft made their final descent into the Pacific Ocean.

science.title: A Single Seamless Mirror: How Japanese Engineers Are Rethinking X-Ray Telescopes From the Ground Up
science.description: A team of Japanese researchers has built an X-ray space telescope with remarkable precision, and they proved it works by launching it aboard a sounding rocket from Alaska in 2024. The telescope, developed through a collaboration between Nagoya University and Japan’s SPring-8 synchrotron radiation facility, flew aboard the FOXSI-4 sounding rocket in April 2024. It […]

science.title: Chandrayaan 2 used to explore water ice on Moon
science.description: Indian scientists have used the observations by the Chandrayaan 1 and Chandrayaan 2 spacecraft to explore water ice on the Moon. The research has implications for future lunar exploration.

science.title: Fungal Alliances: New Research Reveals Co-infection Dynamics In Wheat Diseases
science.description: Australian researchers have revealed the intricate relationship between two major fungal wheat diseases – septoria nodorum blotch and yellow leaf spot – and how the order of infection can dramatically influence disease severity and plant resistance.

science.title: Lunar soil samples from Chang'e missions reveal history of organic matter
science.description: Explore the latest discoveries about lunar soil from China's Chang'e missions, unveiling its history of organic matter in the solar system.

science.title: National medal for backyard astronomer Chris Stockdale who helped discover 100 exoplanets
science.description: From a backyard observatory in regional Victoria, Gippsland astronomer Chris Stockdale spends his nights helping confirm distant planets by tracking tiny changes in starlight that reveal new worlds beyond our solar system.

ai.title: AI overload at work? 5 ways to keep brain engaging
ai.description: Experts caution that excessive AI use may weaken thinking skills over time

ai.title: How ClearTax is using AI Agents to simplify tax filing in India
ai.description: Clear's CTO Suvesh Malhotra highlights the importance of cultural commitment and infrastructure in achieving AI success. After two years of development, the company launched AI tools, including a WhatsApp tax-filing service, demonstrating that genuine utility in AI requires more than just hype.

ai.title: OpenAI to open first permanent London office in 2027
ai.description: OpenAI said on Monday it has secured its first permanent office in London, expanding capacity to meet growing demand in the UK and building on the ChatGPT maker's plans to make the city its largest...

ai.title: US Man Dies By Suicide After Believing AI Wife Asked Him to 'Join' Her In Digital World: "I'm Ready, My Love"
ai.description: The death of Jonathan Gavalas has triggered serious concerns about the psychological risks of prolonged interaction with advanced AI systems like Google Gemini.

ai.title: HKCGI and Wizpresso Release Market‑Wide Study on AI Governance Among HKEX Issuers: Analysis of Over 2,500 Listed Company Disclosures Reveals Significant Gap Between AI Adoption and Governance Readines
ai.description: **media[1010401]**HONG KONG, April 13, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Hong Kong Chartered Governance Institute (HKCGI) and Wizpresso today jointly released a new research white paper titled 'Bridging Innovation and Oversight: Five Governance Priorities - The State of AI Adoption and Governance amongst Hong Kong-listed companies.' Drawing on empirical analysis of more than 2,500 HKEX-listed companies' latest annual and ESG reports, the study provides one of the most comprehensive market-wide assessments to date of artificial intelligence (AI) adoption and governance readiness in Hong Kong's listed sector.
